_assumptions: Using a single-page layout with three distinct sections rather than separate pages for simplicity and fast rendering
Each section is represented as a screen object with components that would be rendered in sequence on a single scrollable page
Health Check section appears first as it addresses the primary 'is this configured?' question
Components include both data display elements and interactive elements (links, status indicators)
Status indicators use a simple pass/fail/pending model with visual state properties
The capabilities list component sources data from a shared registry to prevent drift from CLI reality
Documentation section provides both direct links and optional inline rendering capability
Error states are represented as component variants rather than separate screens
All screens load simultaneously on dashboard initialization to meet the <2 second render requirement
Navigation between sections is implicit (scroll-based) rather than explicit (click-based routing)
Component 'type' field uses common UI pattern names (status-indicator, list, link-group, etc.)
Data fields specify the source and transformation but never include actual secret values
Each component includes an optional 'errorState' property describing failure rendering
The schema prioritizes read-only display components consistent with the no-write requirement

screens.name: health-check
screens.purpose: Display environment configuration status and API connectivity to answer 'is this configured correctly?'

components.name: section-title
components.description: Section heading for environment and connectivity status

components.name: tool-version
components.label: Tool Version
components.errorState: Shows 'Unknown' if version cannot be determined

components.name: environment-variables
components.label: Required Environment Variables

items.name: anthropic-api-key-status
items.label: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
items.securityNote: Never display actual value, only presence/absence

items.name: figma-vars-status
items.label: FIGMA_* Variables
items.note: Future enforcement - may show as optional in current implementation
items.errorState: Yellow indicator with 'Not set (not yet required)' label

items.name: github-vars-status
items.label: GITHUB_* Variables
items.note: Future enforcement - may show as optional in current implementation
items.errorState: Yellow indicator with 'Not set (not yet required)' label

components.errorState: Shows error banner 'Unable to check environment configuration' if check fails entirely

components.name: api-connectivity
components.label: Anthropic API Connectivity
components.pendingState: Gray indicator with 'Checking...' label during initial load

components.name: overall-status
components.successState: Green banner: 'Environment configured correctly - ready to run feature-launchpad'
components.partialState: Yellow banner: 'Some configuration items need attention'
components.errorState: Red banner: 'Environment not configured - see details above'

screens.name: capabilities
screens.purpose: List available CLI commands and pipeline stages with implementation status to answer 'what can this tool do?'

components.name: cli-commands
components.label: CLI Commands
components.note: Must source from same registry used by CLI to prevent drift

itemStructure.name: Command name or invocation pattern
itemStructure.description: One-line description of what the command does

components.errorState: Unable to load CLI commands list

components.name: pipeline-stages
components.label: Pipeline Stages
components.note: Must source from same registry used by pipeline executor to prevent drift

itemStructure.name: Stage identifier (e.g., 'Stage 1', 'Stage 2')
itemStructure.description: One-line description of what the stage does

statusIndicators.implemented: Green badge or checkmark for Stages 1-3
statusIndicators.not_yet_implemented: Gray badge or placeholder icon for Stages 4+

components.errorState: Unable to load pipeline stages list

components.name: cli-usage-guidance
components.content: To run these commands and stages, use the CLI. This dashboard is read-only.
components.linkText: See Documentation section below for CLI usage instructions

screens.name: documentation
screens.purpose: Provide access to project documentation for detailed guidance

components.name: section-title
components.description: Section heading for documentation links and content

links.name: readme-link
links.label: README.md
links.description: Project overview and quick start guide
links.errorState: Shows 'Documentation file not found: README.md' if file missing

links.name: user-manual-link
links.label: USER_MANUAL.md
links.description: Detailed usage instructions and CLI reference
links.errorState: Shows 'Documentation file not found: USER_MANUAL.md' if file missing

components.name: doc-excerpts
components.label: Quick Reference
components.note: Optional inline rendering of documentation excerpts
components.errorState: Section hidden if files cannot be read or parsed

components.name: cli-reminder
components.content: Remember: All feature generation and pipeline execution must be done via the CLI. This dashboard does not create, modify, or execute any operations.

globalComponents.name: initial-load
globalComponents.timeoutBehavior: Display timeout error banner and show partial results

globalComponents.name: section-scroll-tracker
globalComponents.purpose: Optional visual indicator of which section is currently in view

environmentVariableCheck.securityConstraint: Never read or display actual values

apiConnectivityCheck.constraint: Reuse existing HTTP client, do not create new client

capabilitiesRegistry.constraint: Must be single source of truth shared with CLI to prevent drift

documentationFiles.errorHandling: Graceful degradation if files not found

renderingConstraints.performanceTarget: Render complete dashboard in under 2 seconds
renderingConstraints.networkCalls: Only one: Anthropic API reachability check
renderingConstraints.fileSystemAccess: Read-only: environment variables, documentation files, version metadata
renderingConstraints.writeOperations: None permitted - dashboard is fully read-only
renderingConstraints.dependencies: No new runtime dependencies beyond Python standard library

errorHandling.missingEnvironmentVariables: Display as 'Not set' with warning indicator, guide to documentation
errorHandling.apiUnreachable: Display as 'Unreachable' with error indicator, no automatic retry
errorHandling.documentationNotFound: Display 'Documentation file not found' message for affected files
errorHandling.capabilitiesLoadFailure: Display 'Unable to load capabilities list' error in Capabilities section
errorHandling.renderTimeout: Show timeout error banner and display partial results if available
errorHandling.generalError: Display user-friendly error message, avoid exposing technical details
